item.title: ☀️ Hanwha Qcells launches first fully onshore US solar supply chain
item.description: South Korean company begins solar cell production in the US, establishing the country’s first integrated supply chain amid push for clean energy independence.

item.title: 🇵🇸 UN report documents systematic rights violations against Palestinians
item.description: UN inquiry finds civilians in Gaza and West Bank subjected to severe rights violations by Israeli forces and settlers, as tensions escalate in the region.

item.title: 🚁 US Navy sea drones rescue downed helicopter crew in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Task Force 59 drones rescue two Army helicopter pilots, spotlighting the Navy's unmanned capabilities in the strategic waterway amid Iran tensions.

item.title: 🇩🇰 Denmark fails to disclose soaring F-35 costs, auditors say
item.description: Danish State Auditors criticize Defense Ministry for not properly informing lawmakers about the true price of one of the country's largest defense purchases.

item.title: ⚡ Japanese-Canadian consortium to buy European wind and solar farms for AI data centers
item.description: Mitsubishi HC Capital partners with Brookfield to acquire renewable energy plants in Europe to power growing AI infrastructure demands.

item.title: 🚁 Trump says Iran downed US Apache helicopter near Strait of Hormuz, vows response
item.description: President Trump announced Iran shot down a US Army Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and declared the US "must, of necessity, respond." The two pilots were rescued unharmed by a Navy sea drone in what marks the first such rescue operation for the US military.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU proposes 21st sanctions package targeting Russian banks and crypto networks
item.description: The European Union proposed its 21st round of sanctions against Russia, heavily targeting banks, crypto networks, drone production, and oil traders. EU chief diplomat Kaja Kallas announced the sweeping measures aimed at increasing pressure on Moscow over the Ukraine war.

item.title: ⬇️ Trump approval near record low as Americans brace for higher gas prices
item.description: A Reuters poll shows only 22% of Americans approve of Trump's handling of the cost of living, with most expecting gasoline prices to rise further amid the Iran conflict. The finding underscores political pressure on the administration as the Middle East crisis drags on.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Cholera outbreak in Nigeria's Borno state kills 74, infects over 7,000
item.description: A cholera outbreak in northeastern Nigeria's conflict-ridden Borno state has killed 74 people and infected more than 7,000 others since May. The outbreak adds to the humanitarian crisis in a region already devastated by years of insurgency.

item.title: 🐟 Russia faces cod export ban under proposed EU sanctions
item.description: Russian cod could be banned from EU markets in the latest sanctions package aimed at pressuring Moscow to end its invasion of Ukraine. The measure would target one of Russia's key export industries and further strain EU-Russia trade relations.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Strongmen wrong to loathe Europe, The Economist argues
item.description: An analysis challenges the contempt of Trump, Xi, and Putin for European soft power, arguing Europe's economic and diplomatic leverage remains formidable despite their dismissive rhetoric.

item.title: 🚀 NASA names Artemis III crew for next step toward Moon landing
item.description: The space agency announced the four astronauts who will crew the Artemis III mission in 2027, marking the next major milestone in America's return to lunar exploration.

item.title: ⚔️ Middle East crisis live: US helicopter downed as fragile Iran-Israel truce holds
item.description: Comprehensive live coverage of escalating tensions after Iran shot down a US Apache helicopter, with evacuation orders in Lebanon and diplomatic efforts to prevent wider conflict.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel Uses Sound of Crying Children to Lure Civilians in South Lebanon, Report Claims
item.description: Israeli drones in southern Lebanon are allegedly using recordings of crying children to lure civilians into the open, according to a Middle East Eye investigation. The tactic raises serious concerns about violations of international humanitarian law.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Microsoft Cuts Hundreds of Cloud Jobs in China as Data Laws Tighten
item.description: Microsoft is laying off hundreds of employees in its China cloud operations as both Beijing and Washington intensify data security regulations, signaling growing tech decoupling between the two powers.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU to Invest €5 Billion in North Africa, Middle East Renewables
item.description: The European Union plans major investments in renewable energy infrastructure across North Africa and the Middle East, aiming to harness African sunlight to power Europes green transition while reducing energy dependence on Russia.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Xi Jinping Visits North Korean War Monument, Emphasizes Eternal Ties
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping visited a North Korean war monument, evoking what state media called eternal historical memory between the two nations, in a symbolic gesture amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Fed to Hold Rates Through 2026 as War-Driven Inflation Persists
item.description: The US Federal Reserve will keep interest rates unchanged for the rest of 2026, according to a Reuters poll of economists. War-induced inflation has proven more persistent than expected, delaying rate cuts.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Prepares $295 Billion AI Infrastructure Buildout to Challenge US
item.description: Beijing is planning to invest 2 trillion yuan over five years to build data centers across China, intensifying the AI race with the United States. The massive investment aims to strengthen China's AI capabilities.

item.title: 🇰🇪 Kenyan Protester Killed at Demonstration Against US Ebola Facility
item.description: A protester was shot dead during clashes in central Kenya over a US-built Ebola quarantine center. The facility, designed for Americans exposed to Ebola, faced local opposition despite court orders halting construction.

item.title: 🌐 Chinese Hackers Pose Biggest Espionage Threat to Tech Firms, Report Warns
item.description: China-linked hackers represent the greatest espionage danger to technology companies over the past year, according to CrowdStrike. The threat has intensified amid surging AI investment.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland Must Be Involved in Ukraine Peace Talks, Says PM Tusk
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk stated Poland should participate in discussions to end the war in Ukraine, following a London meeting of British, German, and French leaders with President Zelenskyy supporting ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: 🇧🇬 Bulgaria's New Government Ends Military Aid to Ukraine
item.description: Bulgaria's newly appointed defense minister announced the country will no longer provide arms to Ukraine and urged Moscow and Kyiv to negotiate, marking a significant shift in European support for Ukraine.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Faces 'Eye-Watering' Cuts to Fund Defense Pledges
item.description: Britain must make deep public sector cuts to fund defense spending commitments, according to the author of the UK's refreshed defense strategy, calling on Labour MPs to accept major trade-offs.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel Strikes Historic Port of Tyre in Lebanon, Killing Eight
item.description: Israeli forces struck the southern Lebanese city of Tyre, killing at least eight people in an escalation that adds strain to peace efforts in the broader Middle East conflict.
